text.title: Terraform Project Structure and GitHub Actions CI/CD for AWS Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Dev terraform apply nukes a prod resource because state is shared.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used a single state file for all envs to 'save complexity'.
antipattern.fix: Split into per-env dirs with separate state keys. Move resources carefully via `terraform state mv` then re-apply per env.

antipattern.symptom: AWS keys committed to GitHub secrets; leaked; account compromised.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied keys to a secret because OIDC felt 'complex'.
antipattern.fix: Set up OIDC trust policy + role per env. Delete the static keys. Add a pre-commit hook blocking `aws_access_key_id` in repo.

antipattern.symptom: Cost-allocation reports show 60% 'Untagged'; FinOps team blocked from chargeback.
antipattern.root-cause: Author tagged some resources manually; many forgotten.
antipattern.fix: Add default_tags to provider. Re-apply (no-op for resources, just adds tags).

antipattern.symptom: Dev apply fails mid-run; prod apply already started; cross-env race introduces drift.
antipattern.root-cause: max-parallel not set; matrix runs all envs simultaneously.
antipattern.fix: Set strategy.max-parallel: 1. Add environment approval gate on prod.
